title: Causal Temporal Point-Process Pit Model
summary: Pit stops are event sequences with a strong refractory period right after a stop and rising hazard as stint age accumulates.
rationale: Pit stops are event sequences with a strong refractory period right after a stop and rising hazard as stint age accumulates. Temporal point-process models are built for this pattern and can capture nonlinear dependence on event history better than static tabular rows.
implementation_hint: Create one sequence per driver-race ordered by lap. Use marks such as compound, tyre life, stint, lap delta, degradation, and pit indicators; predict next event intensity for a pit on the next lap. Start with a lightweight Hawkes/Transformer-Hawkes style expert or approximate it with a causal sequence encoder that outputs next-lap hazard.
expected_effect: Could uncover signal missed by row-wise boosters, especially around cooldown, repeated stops, and long-range sequence context.
risk: Higher implementation complexity and more fragile validation; needs strict race-driver causal ordering and group-wise CV.
